title: AB-1 Landspeeder

The '''AB-1 Landspeeder''' was a compact civilian [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. Built for tight urban spaces and quick maneuvering rather than cross-country travel, it seated a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and one passenger and was regarded as a practical choice for [[City/Legends|city]] use.

==Characteristics==
No-frills yet sporty,<ref name="ConsumerReport" /> the AB-1 featured a compact design built around a two-seat open [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]].<ref name="AB1Item" /> Its stubby [[Hull/Legends|hull]] sloped sharply downward at the nose, a shape intended to improve aerodynamic stability. A [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]] pod was mounted on each side of the fuselage, giving the [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] its signature appearance.<ref name="ConsumerReport" />

While not the fastest [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] of its era, the AB-1 offered balanced performance well-suited to its role as an urban runabout. Its responsive acceleration and climbing ability allowed it to handle sharp inclines and quick bursts of speed. In terms of overall capability, the AB-1 fell below high-end speeders such as the [[AV-21 Landspeeder|AV-21]] and the [[BARC speeder/Legends|BARC speeder]], but compared favorably with common civilian models like the [[X-34 landspeeder/Legends|X-34]] and [[XP-38 sport landspeeder|XP-38]]. Its small footprint and agile handling made it a popular choice for navigating tight spaces and [[City/Legends|city]] streets.<ref name="ConsumerReport" />

In practice, the AB-1 Landspeeder carried two beings,<ref name="ConsumerReport" /> a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and a passenger,<ref name="AB1Item" /> and were obtainable through trade, vendor and bazaar purchases,<ref name="ConsumerReport" /> or by crafting through [[Artisan/Legends|Master Artisans]]<ref name="MPV">{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=Multi-Passenger%20Vehicles|text=Multi-Passenger Vehicles}}</ref> and [[Engineer/Legends|Engineering]] [[Merchant/Legends|Traders]].<ref name="ConsumerReport" /> Construction required a variety of components, including ferrous and non-ferrous metals, chemicals, and sub-assemblies such as a [[Repulsor field/Legends|repulsor field]] [[Repulsorlift generator/Legends|generator]], stabilization frame, and avionics systems.<ref name="AB1Item" /> Owners could visually customize their vehicles using a [[Vehicle Customization Kit]], while damaged speeders could be repaired with a [[Vehicle Restoration Kit]].<ref name="ConsumerReport" />

==History==
The AB-1 designation dated back to the [[Time/Legends|time]] before the formation of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], when the original [[AB-1 "floatcoach"]] served as a prototype for what would eventually evolve into the modern version of the landspeeder.<ref name="FR">{{WEGCite|story=Field Report|book=The Last Command Sourcebook|reprint=1}}</ref>

By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the AB-1 had evolved into a contemporary production model. Compact and reliable, it was a popular urban speeder with balanced performance, available to citizens across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] through open market trade and local vendors.<ref name="ConsumerReport" />
